response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong web/full-stack and some systems-level background, but the role requires deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His hands-on C++ work is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a university thesis; his recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. He shows no exposure to MQTT, message brokers in industrial contexts, IEC 62443/SDL, or industrial protocols, and C++ is notably absent from his own skills list. Overall a mismatch for a senior C++ industrial-automation position despite solid general engineering credentials.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and desktop
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Strong engineering-practice background: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
concerns: Minimal and dated professional C++ experience (~7 months in 2014-2015); C++ not listed in current skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.)
Recent career trajectory (TypeScript/Node full-stack, AI tooling) points away from the C++ systems focus of this role
